item.title: Australia Passes Law to Trade, Stock Fuel Alongside Rare Earths
item.description: Australia’s parliament passed legislation empowering the country’s export credit agency to physically buy, stockpile and sell fuels alongside critical minerals including rare earths, as the country suffers energy shortages triggered by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Singapore Gets Robotaxis as Grab, WeRide Launch Driverless Cars
item.description: Singapore’s Grab Holdings Ltd. became Southeast Asia’s first ride provider to start a driverless service, betting the technology can help cut costs and prove out robotaxis in dense urban traffic.

item.title: US to Send Dozens of Farm Companies to Philippine Trade Mission
item.description: The US Department of Agriculture will lead dozens of American farm companies to the Philippines in a trade mission this month, aiming to expand access to the Southeast Asian nation.

item.title: RBI’s FX Curbs Jolt Indian Bonds, Bank Stocks, Offshore Dollar Market
item.description: Hedging rupee weakness offshore is less viable for foreign investors.

item.title: BlackRock Grows Australia Wealth Fund Mandate 74% in Two Years
item.description: BlackRock Inc. has emerged as the biggest winner from the expanding alternatives portfolio of Australia’s sovereign wealth fund, with its mandate growing 74% over two years.

item.title: Samsung, SK Hynix Lift Korean Stocks as Iran War-End Hopes Grow
item.description: Chipmakers drove a rebound in South Korean stocks, as hopes that the Iran war may be nearing an end revived risk appetite.

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Releases Source Code for Claude AI Agent
item.description: Anthropic PBC inadvertently released source code for its popular Claude AI agent, raising questions about its operational security and sending developers on a search for clues about the startup’s plans.

item.title: Perplexity AI Machine Accused of Sharing Data With Meta, Google
item.description: Perplexity AI Inc. was accused in a lawsuit of surreptitiously sharing the personal information of its users with Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google in violation of California privacy laws.

item.title: Japan Shares Climb as Trump’s Iran Comments, Tankan Boost Mood
item.description: Japanese shares rebounded after Donald Trump signaled the US aims to end its war with Iran within the next three weeks. Better-than-expected results from Japan’s Tankan business survey also boosted sentiment.

item.title: Chinese Factory Activity Slips as War Lifts Exporters’ Costs
item.description: China’s factory activity slowed in March for export-oriented firms as their costs surged, according to a private survey, contrasting with an official gauge that showed manufacturing improving despite the Iran war.

item.title: Subprime Lender Goeasy Sees Elevated Writeoffs Before Business Improves
item.description: Subprime lender Goeasy Ltd., which surprised investors weeks ago with a surge of bad debts in its vehicle financing business, said it expects loan writeoffs to remain elevated for a while before getting better later in the year.

item.title: Indonesia Offers Three-Year Timeline to Lift Public Float to 15%
item.description: Indonesia will give some listed firms up to three years to raise their public float to at least 15% as part of reforms to boost transparency.

item.title: Asia Manufacturing Holds Up But Cracks Show as War Lifts Prices
item.description: Manufacturing across much of Asia expanded in March, with activity improving in South Korea, Malaysia and Thailand, even as other economies saw the impact of energy supply disruptions from the US-Israeli war on Iran that have pushed oil prices higher.

item.title: Singapore Home Prices Rise at Slower Pace Despite Ongoing Boom
item.description: Singapore private home prices are set to rise at a slower pace in the first quarter of the year, despite continued demand for new homes in the city-state.

item.title: India’s Rupee Seen Sliding to 100 per Dollar as Oil Prices Surge
item.description: India’s rupee may weaken to a record 100 per dollar or beyond if the Iran war drags on, with strategists warning that authorities’ efforts to slow its roughly 10% drop over the past year may only provide temporary relief.

item.title: Fund Beating 99% of Peers Bets Big on Taiwan’s Smaller AI Stocks
item.description: A fund focusing on smaller Taiwanese firms in the AI supply chain has emerged as a big winner, with a diversified strategy helping it weather the volatility that has rattled the industry over the past year.

item.title: CFTC ‘Watching’ Oil Futures Trading Spikes, Agency Official Says
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is monitoring trading in the oil futures market for unusual activity, its enforcement chief said Tuesday.

item.title: Chinese Share Buybacks Surge as Iran War Jolts Global Markets
item.description: China’s listed companies announced the most share buybacks in almost a year last month as the escalating war in Iran drove down stock prices.

item.title: China’s New AI Stocks Drive Volatility in Asia as Hype Grows
item.description: Chinese artificial‑intelligence firms have emerged as one of the most volatile pockets of Asia’s equity markets, with shares of newly listed model developers and chip designers swinging on retail flows.

item.title: Brazil’s Braskem Said to Plan Court Protection Against Creditors
item.description: Braskem SA, Brazil’s largest petrochemical company, is considering seeking protection in local courts against its creditors, according to people involved in the talks.

item.title: South Korea Exports Continue to Surge Despite Escalating Iran Risks
item.description: South Korea’s exports continued to surge in March as robust semiconductor demand helped cushion the economy even as external risks intensify amid the war in Iran.

item.title: Google Readies Screenless Fitbit Band to Rival Growing Whoop Craze
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is developing its own fitness band, aiming to compete with screenless wearable devices from upstarts like Whoop Inc. and Oura Health Oy.

item.title: Japan’s Manufacturer Mood Improves, Buoying BOJ Stance
item.description: Japan’s large manufacturers showed greater confidence in the state of business conditions for a fourth straight quarter even as the Middle East crisis erupted, an outcome that supports the Bank of Japan’s policy stance of continuing to raise interest rates.

item.title: Palmer Luckey-Backed Nuclear Startup Valar Lands $2 Billion Valuation
item.description: Valar Atomics, the nuclear energy startup backed by Anduril Industries Inc. founder Palmer Luckey and Palantir Technologies Inc. executive Shyam Sankar, has raised fresh capital at a valuation of $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CFTC Top Cop Warns Against Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
item.description: A key Commodity Futures Trading Commission official said the agency will use its powers to root out insider trading in prediction markets, as concerns about suspicious activity on the exchanges grow.

item.title: Iran War Unwinds a Year of Investor Bets on Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: A year since President Donald Trump tripped up global financial markets with his so-called Liberation Day tariffs, the investment playbook his move inspired has been firmly put on the shelf.

item.title: Singapore Weighs Adding Gold Storage for Global Central Banks
item.description: Singapore is considering additional gold storage space to accommodate other central banks’ holdings, as part of a broader push to establish the nation as an international bullion hub.

item.title: Australia Manufacturing Slips to Contraction on War Disruptions
item.description: Australia’s manufacturing sector posted its first contraction in five months in March as demand weakened and cost pressures surged amid the escalating war in the Middle East.

item.title: Optimism for Iran War Offramp Set to Lift Asia Markets
item.description: TEHRAN, IRAN - MARCH 02: A general view of Tehran with smoke visible in the distance after explosions were reported in the city, on March 02, 2026 in Tehran, Iran. (Photo by Contributor/Getty Images)

item.title: Energy Shock Spurs Caution as Asia Bankers Fund Data Center Boom
item.description: The energy shock sparked by the Iran war is increasingly coming up in conversations about data center deals for Asian bankers, who have helped finance billions of dollars of artificial intelligence infrastructure across the region.
